name: Digital Product Passport (DPP)
thesis: Is the EU Digital Product Passport becoming real on schedule - on the regulator's side, and on industry's side?

scores.regulator_delivery_note: Of everything the EU committed to deliver by now, how much landed - and on time. Computed over milestones that are due, weighted. The registry (weight 3) delivered one day late and scores half credit; the service-provider act (weight 2) is missed and scores zero. That is 1.5 of 5 weighted points, or 30 of 100.

corrections.text: We previously graded the registry call as a miss, claiming the implementing regulation was unadopted. Wrong. It was adopted 16 July and published 17 July, before our post. Cause: reliance on a pre-adoption tracker treated as current. Remedy, effective now: no call changes state without an Official Journal or EUR-Lex citation, and no miss is called without a same-day EUR-Lex query. Tally corrected to two resolved, one delivered late, one missed.

this_week_read.headline: The DPP Registry went live one day past its legal deadline. We had called it a miss; that was wrong, and we have corrected the record.
this_week_read.body: The EU Central DPP Registry is real. Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/1778 was adopted on 16 July 2026 and published in the Official Journal on 17 July 2026, entering into force on 6 August 2026; the registry, its testing environment and user guidelines went live on 20 July 2026, one day after the 19 July legal deadline, with registration mechanics phasing in by sector. Under this instrument's method a delivery one day late scores half credit, not zero, so regulator delivery moves from 0 to 30. Our 19 July post graded the registry a miss on the premise that the implementing rules were still unadopted. They were not: they had been adopted on 16 July, before we posted. We have logged the correction and its cause on the record and tightened the rule - no call changes state without an Official Journal or EUR-Lex citation. Separately, the service-provider delegated act remains missed: its 30 June 2026 deadline passed with no adoption, and the Commission's indicative timeline now points to Q2 2027.

milestones.label: DPP Registry operational and accepting registrations
milestones.note: Delivered one day late. Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/1778 was adopted 16 July 2026 and published in the Official Journal 17 July 2026 (in force 6 August 2026); the registry, its testing environment and user guidelines went live 20 July 2026, one day after the 19 July legal deadline. Registration mechanics phase in by sector.

milestones.label: DPP system standards published (EN)
milestones.note: EN 18216, 18219-18223 published 25 June 2026; the last two, prEN 18239 and prEN 18246 (security/confidentiality and data authentication), were under formal vote until 16 July 2026 and are not yet published as EN.

milestones.label: Standards harmonised (cited in the Official Journal)
milestones.note: Publication is not harmonisation. OJ citation, which creates the presumption of conformity, is still pending.

milestones.label: Customs interconnection live
milestones.note: Four years after the registry rules enter into force.

milestones.label: DPP service-provider delegated act adopted
milestones.note: Deadline 30 June 2026 passed with the delegated act (requirements for DPP service providers plus a conformity-assessment scheme) not adopted. The Commission's indicative timeline now points to Q2 2027.

milestones.label: Iron and steel ESPR delegated act adopted
milestones.note: First priority product group; not yet adopted, timelines drifting.

milestones.label: Textiles, tyres, aluminium ESPR acts adopted
Mattresses and ICT ESPR acts adopted
Energy-related product rules updated
milestones.note: Rolling range, tracked as ongoing progress.

signals.name: Active DPP pilots across value chains
Public DPP solutions / vendors live
signals.source: Market mapping / CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice
signals.note: ~15 established public DPP solution vendors (Circularise, Kezzler, Scantrust, Spherity, OPTEL, Protokol and peers); the CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice now lists 111 participants.

signals.name: GS1 Digital Link adoption proxy
signals.note: GS1 Sunrise 2027 - the 2D / Digital Link migration most DPP carriers ride on - is in pilot across 48 countries representing 88% of world GDP.

signals.name: Implementable standards (published as EN)
signals.note: Of the 8 JTC 24 areas, how many are stable enough to build against today.

calls.statement: The EU DPP Registry will be operational and accepting registrations by its legal deadline of 19 July 2026.
calls.grading_note: Delivered, one day late. Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/1778 was adopted 16 July 2026, published in the Official Journal 17 July 2026, and enters into force 6 August 2026. The registry, its testing environment and user guidelines went live 20 July 2026, one day after the 19 July legal deadline; registration mechanics phase in by sector. We first graded this a miss on 19 July on the premise that the implementing regulation was unadopted; it had in fact been adopted on 16 July. Corrected 20 July 2026.

calls.statement: The DPP service-provider delegated act will be adopted by 30 June 2026.
calls.grading_note: The 30 June 2026 deadline passed with the delegated act on DPP service providers still a draft, not adopted. The Commission's indicative timeline now points to Q2 2027.

calls.statement: All eight JTC 24 DPP system standards will be published as European Standards by 30 September 2026.
At least one DPP standard will be cited in the EU Official Journal (presumption of conformity) by 31 December 2026.
The iron and steel ESPR delegated act will be adopted within 2026.
The textiles ESPR delegated act will not be adopted before 2027.

methodology: Track A scores the EU's own delivery against the dates the regulation set, over milestones that are due only, weighted (registry and the service-provider act count most). delivered_on_time = 1.0, delivered_late = 0.5, slipped/missed = 0, upcoming is excluded until its date passes. Track B is a provisional proxy of industry readiness and is tagged as such until the proprietary onboarding signal replaces the basket. Calls are falsifiable, dated, and graded HIT, DELIVERED LATE or MISS in public, with a running record. Corrections are dated, permanent and shown on the record; no call changes state without an Official Journal or EUR-Lex citation.

source.line: Sources: Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 (ESPR); Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/1778 (DPP Registry); European Commission DPP FAQ v1.0 (DG GROW); CEN-CENELEC JTC 24; EUR-Lex. Verified 20 July 2026.
